articles.title: Madison Square Garden Sues Wired Magazine Over L.G.B.T.Q. Tracking Report
articles.description: The Manhattan venue claims a July article from the technology magazine falsely implied that it tracked information about some celebrities’ sexual orientation for “discriminatory purposes.”

articles.title: JD Vance’s Remarks on Epstein and Israel Echo Conspiracy Theories
articles.description: The vice president’s comment that there were ties between Mr. Epstein and the “highest levels” of Israeli intelligence troubled some in his party.

articles.title: What to Know About Fires Caused by Lightning in the Pacific Northwest
articles.description: Dozens of fires ignited in Oregon and Washington as more than 14,000 lightning strikes hit the region during thunderstorms this week.

articles.title: Why Jordan Is Becoming a New Focus in the U.S.-Iran War
articles.description: Four Iranian attacks in five days have targeted U.S. troops in Jordan, U.S. officials said, including one on Friday that killed two soldiers and left another service member missing.

articles.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
articles.description: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

articles.title: America Caught World Cup Fever. His Job Is to Capitalize on It.

articles.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
articles.description: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

articles.title: Wildfire Smoke Hangs Over World Cup Final
articles.description: Smoke has blanketed New York City and other parts of the Northeast United States in a thick haze this week, unleashing smoggy skies and dangerous air.

articles.title: Mamdani Says He May Still Order Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu’s Arrest in NYC
articles.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said in an interview with The New York Times that he was in “an active conversation” with New York City’s Law Department on whether he had the authority to arrest the Israeli leader.

articles.title: Cindy Burbank, Nebraska’s Democratic Senate Nominee, Files to Withdraw From Race
articles.description: The move by Cindy Burbank could clear a path for Dan Osborn, an independent candidate backed by her party’s leadership. But Republicans have cried foul, and her effort could face obstacles.

articles.title: Lillian Roberts, Crusading New York Labor Leader, Dies at 98
articles.description: She unionized state hospitals and battled with Mayor Michael R. Bloomberg during her feisty leadership of the nation’s largest municipal workers union.

articles.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
articles.description: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

articles.title: They Called Sam Neill ‘Skux.’ (It Was a Compliment.)
articles.description: The New Zealand slang word, dating back to the 1990s, was pulled back into circulation in tributes to the actor, who died on Tuesday.

articles.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
articles.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

articles.title: Wildfires Continue To Burn in Ontario, Canada, Sending Smoke to U.S. Cities
articles.description: Nearly 200 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province as smoke continued to drift across parts of Canada and the United States.

articles.title: Mike Lindell Not Registered to Vote in Minnesota, Where He is Running for Governor
articles.description: President Trump endorsed Mike Lindell, a leader of the election denial movement, for Minnesota governor this week.

articles.description: Fanatics has become one of the most influential companies in sports, building out a massive merchandise, collectibles and sports gambling platform.

articles.description: Overnight strikes damaged a tunnel and three bridges in Hormozgan Province, the governor’s office said.

articles.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live

articles.title: As Trump Scraps With Meloni, His Envoy to Italy Is at Sea
articles.description: Tilman J. Fertitta, the U.S. ambassador to Rome, is summering on his superyacht as President Trump squabbles with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy.

articles.title: Uproar Over Surrogate Baby Prompts German Official’s Sudden Resignation
articles.description: Jens Spahn, a top leader of Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s party, resigned his post after announcing that he had a baby by surrogate, which is illegal in Germany.

articles.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
articles.description: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

articles.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
articles.description: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

articles.title: Trump and Mamdani to Attend World Cup Final on Sunday

articles.title: Scam victims can owe taxes on stolen money. A bill in Congress could offer relief
articles.description: A bill in the House would restore a pre-2018 rule that generally allowed a tax deduction for theft losses and would add other relief for fraud victims.

articles.description: The Israeli authorities have enacted a broad array of countermeasures to contain what they describe as online recruiting efforts by Iranian agents.

articles.title: Most Forest Fires in Canada Are Simply Impossible to Put Out
articles.description: Half of Canada’s wildfires are in remote areas with no road access. Impossible to fight, they are often left to burn.

articles.title: They Don’t Fight Crime, but They Do Eat Bugs: The Bats of N.Y.C.

articles.title: In Bangladesh, Argentina’s Soccer Team Finds a Huge Fan Base

articles.description: As visitors flock to places like Mexico City, Milan and Copenhagen, residents are rethinking what they share, and who they share it with.

articles.description: Prosecutors say Tamara Lich wants a “blank cheque” for international travel, including a cruise, arguing that she is trying to profit from the very protest that got her sentenced.

articles.description: The births are a boon to efforts to revive the species, which was once nearly hunted to extinction.

articles.title: Russia Bill Broadens Sanctions but Could Fuel Fears Over the Dollar
articles.description: The Trump administration has recently sought to scale back sanctions as adversaries develop workarounds that some believe could erode the U.S. dollar’s supremacy.

articles.title: What Will You Do in Retirement? Your Money Depends on It.

articles.title: Iqaluit, Canada’s Most Northern City, Gets Its First Bus Line
articles.description: Jacinto Marques left Angola for Canada’s frozen edge. Now he’s running a bus service in Iqaluit, the largest, and only city in Canada’s Arctic.
